meaning: n.[物](射线的) 轨道,弹道,轨线
examples: Most of all it was the extraordinary trajectory of his life.
But most of all it was the extraordinary trajectory of his life.
Education, and not just knowledge gained on campus, will be a core part of Generation Z's career trajectory.
The clothing business, he believes, is influenced by the same trajectory as artisan food.
Investors might overlook this if the company were otherwise sound and on a growth trajectory.
